sentence: مَرْحَبَا، كِيفَك؟ بِتْمَنَّى تْكُون مَبْسُوط.
raw: مرحبا، كيفك؟ بتمنى تكون مبسوط.
ipa: ˈmarħabaː ˈkiːfak bitˈmannaː ˈtkuːn mabˈsuːtˤ
gloss_en: Hello, how are you? I hope you are well.
notes: kīfak (cowell1964); b-imperfect بِتْمَنَّى; initial cluster tk- (Levantine syncope, almbark2015)

sentence: اَلزَّيت وَالزَّعْتَر عَ الطَّاوْلِة يَا سِتِّي.
raw: الزيت والزعتر ع الطاولة يا ستي.
ipa: azˈzeːt wazˈzaʕtar ˈʕa tˤˈtˤɑːwle ˈjaː ˈsittiː
gloss_en: The oil and the za'atar are on the table, grandma.
notes: zēt u zaʕtar (Palestinian staple collocation); sun assimilation az-z; sittī 'grandma' geminate تّ; diphthong /aj/→[eː] monophthongisation (zēt) per (fadda2016)

sentence: مَا بِدِّيش أَرُوح عَالْمَدْرَسِة بُكْرَا الصُّبِح.
raw: ما بديش أروح عالمدرسة بكرا الصبح.
ipa: ˈmaː bidˈdiːʃ ʔaˈruːħ ʕaːlˈmadrase ˈbukraː ˈɑsˤsˤubiħ
gloss_en: I don't want to go to school tomorrow morning.
notes: circumfix negation mā...š (cotter2016: Gazan/rural -š); imala madrase (fadda2016)

sentence: قَدَّيش صَارُوا التَّلَامِيذ فِي صَفّ الْقُدْس؟
raw: قديش صاروا التلاميذ في صف القدس؟
ipa: ʔadˈdeːʃ ˈsˤɑːruː ttalaːˈmiːd ˈfiː ˈsˤɑff alˈʔuds
gloss_en: How many pupils are there now in the Jerusalem class?
notes: ʔaddēš; ق=/ʔ/ urban (cotter2016 on Gaza City /ʔ/); ذ→/d/ in talāmīd (urban stop reflex)

sentence: جِدِّي زَرَع ظَهْر الْجَبَل زَيْتُون وتِين.
raw: جدي زرع ظهر الجبل زيتون وتين.
ipa: ˈʒiddiː ˈzaraʕ ˈdˤɑhr ˈalʒabal zeːˈtuːn ˈwtiːn
gloss_en: My grandfather planted the hillside with olives and figs.
notes: ج=/ʒ/ urban Palestinian (almbark2015); ظ urban reflex; diphthong ay in زَيْتُون

sentence: رُوح عَ السُّوق وْجِيب خُبِز طَازَة.
raw: روح ع السوق وجيب خبز طازة.
ipa: ˈruːħ ˈʕa sˈsuːʔ ˈwʒiːb ˈxubiz ˈtˤɑːza
gloss_en: Go to the market and bring fresh bread.
notes: imperatives ruh/jib; ʕa+article contraction; urban qaf→ʔ in as-suʔ (cotter2016)

sentence: الدِّنْيَا بَرْد الْيَوْم وْمَا فِي شَمْس.
raw: الدنيا برد اليوم وما في شمس.
ipa: adˈdinjaː ˈbard alˈjoːm ˈwmaː ˈfiː ˈʃams
gloss_en: The weather is cold today and there's no sun.
notes: id-dinya bard 'it's cold (weather)' impersonal (cowell1964); existential negation mā fī 'there isn't' (cowell1964); il-yōm 'today' with /aw/→[oː] monophthong (fadda2016; almbark2015)

sentence: بِكَمّ الْكِيلُو بَنْدُورَة؟ بِعَشَر شَوَاقِل.
raw: بكم الكيلو بندورة؟ بعشر شواقل.
ipa: biˈkamm alˈkiːluː banˈduːra ˈbiʕaʃar ʃaˈwaːʔil
gloss_en: How much is a kilo of tomatoes? Ten shekels.
notes: bikam 'how much' (cowell1964); bandōra 'tomato' Levantine (cowell1964); shawāqil 'shekels' with ق=/ʔ/ urban (cotter2016); kīlū mater spelling كِيلُو

sentence: الْمَوْعِد السَّاعَة أَرْبَعَة الْعَصِر.
raw: الموعد الساعة أربعة العصر.
ipa: alˈmoːʕid asˈsaːʕa ˈʔarbaʕa ˈalʕɑsˤir
gloss_en: The appointment is at four o'clock in the afternoon.
notes: mawʕid→[moːʕid] /aw/→[oː] (fadda2016); is-sāʕa arbaʕa 'four o'clock' (cowell1964); sun assimilation as-s in السَّاعَة

sentence: أَنَا سَاكِن فِي رَام اللَّه مَعَ أَهْلِي.
raw: أنا ساكن في رام الله مع أهلي.
ipa: ˈʔanaː ˈsaːkin ˈfiː ˈraːm ˈallah ˈmaʕa ˈʔahliː
gloss_en: I live in Ramallah with my family.
notes: sākin 'residing/living' active participle (cowell1964); ahl 'family, folks' (cowell1964); Ramallah placename

sentence: لِفّ عَ الْيَمِين وْكَمِّل دُغْرِي.
raw: لف ع اليمين وكمل دغري.
ipa: ˈliff ˈʕa ljaˈmiːn ˈwkammil ˈduɣriː
gloss_en: Turn right and keep going straight.
notes: liff 'turn' and kammil 'continue' imperatives (cowell1964); dughri 'straight ahead' Levantine (cowell1964); ʕa+article elision ʕa l-yamīn

sentence: الْأَكِل كَان زَاكِي كْتِير.
raw: الأكل كان زاكي كتير.
ipa: ˈalʔakil ˈkaːn ˈzaːkiː ˈktiːr
gloss_en: The food was very tasty.
notes: kān past copula (cowell1964); zāki 'tasty' Levantine (cowell1964); ktīr 'very, a lot' with initial cluster (cowell1964)

sentence: الدُّكَّان لِسَّا مَفْتُوح؟ لَأْ، مِش مَفْتُوح.
raw: الدكان لسا مفتوح؟ لأ، مش مفتوح.
ipa: addukˈkaːn ˈlissaː mafˈtuːħ ˈlaʔa ˈmiʃ mafˈtuːħ
gloss_en: Is the shop still open? No, it's not open.
notes: dukkān 'shop', lissa 'still, yet' (cowell1964); predicate negation miš (cowell1964); laʔ 'no'; sun assimilation ad-d in الدُّكَّان

sentence: كَمّ وَلَد عِنْدَك؟
raw: كم ولد عندك؟
ipa: ˈkamm ˈwalad ˈʕindak
gloss_en: How many children do you have?
notes: kam 'how many' + singular count noun walad (cowell1964); ʕindak 'you have' pseudo-verb (cowell1964)

sentence: إِذَا بِتِيجِي بُكْرَا مِنْرُوح عَ الْبَحْر.
raw: إذا بتيجي بكرا منروح ع البحر.
ipa: ˈʔidaː biˈtiːʒiː ˈbukraː minˈruːħ ˈʕa lˈbaħr
gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we'll go to the sea.
notes: iza conditional (cowell1964); b-imperfect bitīji 'you come' with ج=/ʒ/ urban (almbark2015); m(n)-imperfect 1pl minrūḥ (cowell1964); إذا ذ→[d] urban stop reflex (cotter2016)

sentence: رُحِت عَ السُّوق وِشْتَرَيْت خُضَار.
raw: رحت ع السوق وشتريت خضار.
ipa: ˈruħit ˈʕa sˈsuːʔ wiʃtaˈreːt xuˈdˤɑːr
gloss_en: I went to the market and bought vegetables.
notes: ruḥt 1sg perfect (cowell1964); ishtarēt 'I bought' with /aj/→[eː] (fadda2016); ق=/ʔ/ in as-sūʔ (cotter2016); sun assimilation as-s

sentence: رَح نْسَافِر عَ الْأُرْدُن الْأُسْبُوع الْجَاي.
raw: رح نسافر ع الأردن الأسبوع الجاي.
ipa: ˈraħ ˈnsaːfir ˈʕa lˈʔurdun alʔusˈbuːʕ alˈʒaːj
gloss_en: We'll travel to Jordan next week.
notes: raḥ future particle (cowell1964); n-imperfect 1pl nsāfir; il-jāy 'the coming (next)' active participle with ج=/ʒ/ urban (almbark2015)

sentence: أَلُو، مِين مَعِي؟
raw: ألو، مين معي؟
ipa: ˈʔaluː ˈmiːn ˈmaʕiː
gloss_en: Hello, who's this? (on the phone)
notes: alo telephone-opener; mīn 'who' (cowell1964); maʕi 'with me'

sentence: أَهْلَا وْسَهْلَا، نَوَّرْت!
raw: أهلا وسهلا، نورت!
ipa: ˈʔahlaː ˈwsahlaː nawˈwart
gloss_en: Welcome! You've lit up the place.
notes: ahlan wa-sahlan welcome formula; nawwart 'you have brightened (the place)' hospitality response, geminate /ww/ kept unmonophthongized (cowell1964)

sentence: الصَّبِر مِفْتَاح الْفَرَج.
raw: الصبر مفتاح الفرج.
ipa: ˈɑsˤsˤɑbir mifˈtaːħ ˈalfaraʒ
gloss_en: Patience is the key to relief.
notes: proverb iṣ-ṣabr miftāḥ il-faraj; sun assimilation aṣ-ṣ in الصَّبِر; ج=/ʒ/ urban in il-faraj (almbark2015)
